title: fix: pin 9 actions to commit SHA
body:
Re-submission of #11045. Had a problem with my fork and had to delete it, which closed the original PR. Apologies for the noise.

## Summary

This PR pins all GitHub Actions to immutable commit SHAs instead of mutable version tags.

- Pin 9 unpinned actions to full 40-character SHAs
- Add version comments for readability

## Changes by file

| File | Changes |
|------|---------|
| cancel.yml | Pinned actions to SHA |
| locales-coverage.yml | Pinned actions to SHA |
| publish-docker.yml | Pinned actions to SHA |
| semantic-pr-title.yml | Pinned actions to SHA |
| size-limit.yml | Pinned actions to SHA |
| test-coverage-pr.yml | Pinned actions to SHA |

## A note on internal action pinning

This PR pins all actions including org-owned ones. Best practice is to pin everything — the tj-actions/changed-files attack was an internally maintained action that was compromised, and every repo referencing it by tag silently executed attacker code. That said, it's your codebase. If you'd prefer to leave org-owned actions unpinned, let us know and we'll adjust the PR.

## How to verify

Review the diff — each change is mechanical and preserves workflow behavior:
- **SHA pinning**: `action@v3` becomes `action@abc123 # v3` — original version preserved as comment
- No workflow logic, triggers, or permissions are modified

I put up some research on this on [Twitter](https://x.com/vigilance_one/status/2036581210663616729) and a [research site](https://www.vigilantdefense.com/research/github-top-50k-repos-cicd-security-scan) if you want more context. I wrote a scanner called Runner Guard and open sourced it [here](https://github.com/Vigilant-LLC/runner-guard).

If you have any questions, reach out. I'll be monitoring comms.

\- Chris Nyhuis (dagecko)
author: dagecko

title: fix(math): correctly validate second point in isLineSegment
body:
Fixes #11001 

While reviewing the `isLineSegment` implementation, I noticed that it was validating `segment[0]` twice instead of checking both elements of the segment. This allowed invalid segments like `[[0, 0], "invalid"]` to pass validation.

### Changes
- Fixed the validation to correctly check both elements (`segment[0]` and `segment[1]`)
- Added test cases to cover valid and invalid segment scenarios

title: fix: Arrow point index Out-of-Bounds
body:
There are a ton of invariant failures like this in Sentry:
```
There must be a valid lastClickedPoint in order to drag it. selectedPointsIndices([2]) points(0..1) lastClickedPoint(2)
```

This PR turns the invariant into a `console.error()̢`, with a fallback to the last valid arrow point to make the drag operation succeed anyway.

Additionally a pointentially more robust state update during arrow point drag is added where the commit threshold is not triggered.

Additionally, there are some failures in binding restoration for library elements, so the error message now captures the condition variables in Sentry to let us see what triggers the error.

title: feat(editor): allow laser-pointing in view mode
body:
Allow laser pointing in view mode when laser tool enabled. This is mostly to enable host apps to allow this. On excalidraw.com/E+, the only way to do this now is through shortcuts (K).

Disabled when `props.viewModeEnabled==true` for now which is often a special case in which users shouldn't be able to do anything other than move on the canvas. May be changed in the future.

--

Also changes laser/hand behavior in view mode with respect to interacting with embeds - now clicking anywhere on the embed in view mode will interact with it.

/cc @zsviczian

title: fix: reconciliation of server updates & refactor restore
body:
- fix reconciliation after firebase save preferring remote elements to latest local elements, reverting local changes made during the saving process
- factored element version bumping (except for deleted elements, TBD) from inside `restoreElements()` into its own helper `bumpElementVersions()`
- removed `restore` in favor of using the individual `restoreElements()` and `restoreAppState()` to reduce API surface area

title: fix: Clarify welcome screen message about browser storage limitations

Closes: #10664 The previous message "**All your data is saved locally in your browser**" could be misinterpreted as reassurance that data is safe. This update clarifies that: 1. Drawings are saved in browser storage 2. Browser storage can be cleared unexpectedly 3. Users should save to file regularly to avoid data loss This makes it clear that the **auto-save** is not a reliable backup mechanism.
Proof showing the creation/modification is working as expected

body: [Screencast from 2026-01-06 19-08-19.webm](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d75e526e-e1d4-424f-876c-3e2a9ce3984b)
author: mtolmacs

reviews.body:
This enables precise selecting of binding points, which is a great UX improvement! The geometric logic for `hitElementItself` looks solid.

**1. Code Duplication in `App.tsx`**
I noticed very similar logic for "checking focus point hover" is repeated in two places:
1.  Inside `handleCanvasPointerMove` (lines ~6933)
2.  Inside `handleCanvasPointerDown` (lines ~7946)

It might be worth extracting this into a helper function (e.g., `getHoveredFocusPoint`) to reduce duplication and keep `App.tsx` cleaner.

**2. Hit Testing Threshold**
You are using `const hitThreshold = 5 / this.state.zoom.value`.
Does this feel easy enough to click on high zoom levels? (Logic seems correct as it scales with zoom, just checking if 5px is the intended physical screen target size).

Otherwise, LGTM!

title: feat: Non-elbow arrow snapping and behavior changes
body:
This PR implements fixed-point binding for simple (non-elbow) arrows, enabling more intuitive arrow behavior when binding to filled shapes.

## Key Features:
  - Same-element binding: Simple arrows can now bind both endpoints to the same element
  - Fixed relative positioning: When arrow endpoints are drawn inside filled shapes, they maintain their relative position within the bound element as it moves
  - Automatic migration: Existing PointBinding objects are automatically migrated to FixedPointBinding when
  appropriate
  - Enhanced collision detection: Improved logic for detecting when arrow endpoints should use fixed-point binding vs traditional focus/gap binding

## Changes:
  - Enhanced binding logic in packages/element/src/binding.ts with new calculateFixedPointForNonElbowArrowBinding function
  - Added migration logic in packages/excalidraw/data/restore.ts to convert legacy
  bindings
  - Comprehensive test suite covering same-element binding, position maintenance, and edge cases
  - Updated snapshots to reflect the new binding behavior

  This improves the user experience by making arrows behave  more predictably when drawn inside shapes, maintaining
  their visual relationship even when the bound elements are moved or transformed.

## Mark's original notes
1) Arrow start point outside of target shape A:
- End point dragged close to the shape outline from the outside (100%-110%), it snaps and binds at 5px gap. If user waits for 1500ms, then snapping gets disabled and user can set a custom gap and it still binds.
- End point is dragged on the shape A outline or inside shape A, initially it will snap to A at 5px. If user waits 1500ms, then snapping gets disabled and the arrow endpoint gets inside the shape.
- In the latter case when the user releases the mouse button, it will glue the endpoint to a fixed local point inside the shape. (a different kind of "binding").

2) If you start the arrow inside the shape A:
- As long as your end point is inside shape A, it will also "glue" to a local fixed point. Therefore the arrow will move with the shape A when A is dragged (both points are local fixed glued).
- If your end point is dragged to the outline of the shape or inside the binding area of shape A, it will NOT snap and NOT bind. We will not allow binding to the same shape!
- If your endpoint is dragged outside of shape A (remember, no resistance as no snapping), then the start point will be "pushed out" of shape A, snap at 5px and bind. The endpoint then behaves based on the other rules here.
- If you try to drag the endpoint back inside shape A, no resistance because no snapping, just gets back inside.

3) We will add a snapping disabling heuristic based on cursor movement speed, but this might be optional. CtrlCmd will still disable binding AS WELL AS snapping immediately. Once CtrlCmd is released, the end point is pushed out, the timeout restarts.

4) Elbow arrows will always remain outside shapes, except on CtrlCmd, but then they don't bind.

5) Change the z-index when the arrow is inside the shape
6) (Mark suggestion) Unbind the simple arrow if dragged as a singular element to avoid zero-length arrows when inside arrow is dragged outside an element
7) (Mark suggestion) If a simple arrow is not directly pointing to a bindable shape, use fixed point binding, to avoid "jumpy arrow" on dragging the bound element


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

- **New Features**
  - Enhanced arrow and linear element binding behavior, including fixed-point and focus/gap binding modes.
  - Introduced a new binding mode option for more precise control when connecting arrows to other elements.
  - Added timed binding mode transitions for smoother and more intuitive arrow connections.

- **Improvements**
  - Improved dragging and editing interactions for arrows, preventing accidental unbinding and ensuring endpoints avoid overlapping bound elements.
  - Refined binding suggestions and hover detection for more accurate and user-friendly arrow placement.
  - Enhanced multi-point arrow editing and element movement behavior.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - Addressed issues with arrow binding when both ends connect to the same element.
  - Improved stability when moving or resizing bound arrows and elements.

- **Tests**
  - Expanded test coverage for new binding behaviors and interaction scenarios.
  - Updated existing tests to validate enhanced binding logic and rendering behavior.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

packages/element/src/linearElementEditor.ts (1)
`2103-2110`: **Consider improving readability of the binding condition.** The nested condition is correct but could be more readable. ```diff - if ( - isArrowElement(element) && - !( - hoveredElement?.id === otherHoveredElement?.id && - hoveredElement != null - ) && - bindMode === "focus" - ) { + const bothEndsInSameElement = hoveredElement?.id === otherHoveredElement?.id && hoveredElement != null; + if ( + isArrowElement(element) && + !bothEndsInSameElement && + bindMode === "focus" + ) { ```
packages/excalidraw/components/App.tsx (1)
`8822-8825`: **Optimize repeated isElbowArrow calls.** The `isElbowArrow(newElement)` is called multiple times with the same element. ```diff + const isElbow = isElbowArrow(newElement); const hoveredElement = getHoveredElementForBinding( { x: gridX, y: gridY }, this.scene.getNonDeletedElements(), this.scene.getNonDeletedElementsMap(), this.state.zoom, - isElbowArrow(newElement), - isElbowArrow(newElement), + isElbow, + isElbow, ); ```
packages/element/src/binding.ts (1)
`544-610`: **Monitor performance impact of intersection calculations** The extended segment creation and intersection testing in the binding logic could impact performance when dealing with many arrows or frequent updates. Consider adding performance monitoring or optimization strategies: 1. Cache intersection results when possible 2. Use spatial indexing for element lookups if performance be

title: feat: switch between basic shapes
body:
fix https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/8811

This PR brings the ability to quickly switch between selected shapes if they are
- a mix of generic elements (rectangle, diamonds, or ellipses) and other elements
- only linear elements

For elements that can be quickly shape switched, when selected, pressing tab will toggle the shape switch panel. After the panel is opened up, we can either select from the panel by clicking on one of the options or tab to cycle through the available shapes. 

Shape switch panel can be closed when the selection is cleared or when escape key is pressed. 

Also, when a new node is created in a flowchart, we show the hint as well.

title: fix: canvas panning stops when hovering over frame title (#10340)
body:
## Description

Fixes #10340

This PR fixes an issue where canvas panning with two-finger trackpad gestures on macOS would stop when the cursor hovered over a frame title.

## The Bug

On macOS, when using two-finger trackpad gestures to pan the canvas, the canvas movement would stop as soon as the cursor touched a frame's title. Users had to manually move the cursor away from the frame title to continue panning.

**Root Cause:**
The `handleWheel()` function was rejecting wheel events from frame title div elements. Frame titles are rendered as DOM elements (divs with IDs like `${appId}-frame-name-${frameId}`), and when two-finger trackpad gestures generated wheel events over these elements, they were filtered out before reaching the canvas panning logic.

## The Fix

Modified `handleWheel()` to detect frame title divs by checking if the event target's ID contains `-frame-name-` or if the target is inside such an element. When a frame title is detected, the wheel events are now allowed to pass through to the canvas panning logic, just like events from canvas, textarea, or iframe elements.

**Changes:**
- Added frame title detection logic in `handleWheel()` method
- Extended the allowed event targets to include frame title divs
- Minimal change: Only 10 lines added/modified in a single method

## Testing

Tested on macOS with two-finger trackpad gestures:
- Canvas panning continues smoothly when cursor moves over frame titles
- Normal canvas interaction (clicking, dragging) still works correctly
- Frame title editing (double-click) still works as expected
- No regressions in other panning methods (space+drag, hand tool)

Currently, bound text elements within a selected group can be associated with additional containers because the action wrap text in a container does not filter and check for text elements that are already bound to a container.

The reason is that when bound text elements are within a selected group, they are also included as selected elements.

## Fix
Small addition to both the predicate and perform of `actionWrapTextInContainer` to ensure that the action will not be presented to the user within the context menu and will filter out text elements already bound to a container.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bd43f221-a483-41cc-bce4-39c5ecf97fea

### Other alternatives
It could also be possible to prevent the action for any elements that are within a selected group, but wanted to produce a minimal option first and see if there are any suggestions from the team.

## Feedback Welcome
Open to any suggestions, and if there's a better approach, I'm more than willing to change and/or collaborate! @mtolmacs @dwelle 

Fixes #10181
author: cTangonan123

commits.message: fix: only enable wrap text in a container when at least one text element selected is unbound

title: fix: eraser can handle dots without regressing prior performance improvements
body:
## Summary
This PR fixes an issue with the eraser tool where freehand dots were difficult to erase.

The solution works by:
1. **Dot detection**: An element is classified as a dot if its bounding box diagonal is below a certain threshold.
2. **Hit radius calculation**: For dots, we skip path-collision checks and instead compute a `hitRadius` based on the element’s `strokeWidth`.
3. **Erase evaluation**: The eraser checks the distance between its last path point and the element’s center. If this distance is less than or equal to the `hitRadius`, the element is erased.

This approach ensures dots are erased reliably while preserving the previously employed performance enhancement of checking for intersecting paths for all elements larger than dots.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/606b03e4-caf4-43dd-915f-337ba36bd800

### Credits
credits to @Brikaa and @mtolmacs for specifying a more performant solution in treating a dot as a circle as opposed to an outline of its shape.

### Feedback Welcome
More than happy to adjust as needed. If there's anything needed to improve or a more performant approach, I'm open to collaborating. Appreciate any suggestions or review comments!

cc @mtolmacs @dwelle – would appreciate any feedback you guys may have on this one!

Fixes #9762 
Issues #9904 #9918 #9932 seem to all be concerned with the same behavior and could be closed as well.
author: cTangonan123

title: fix: bound text rotation across alignments
body:
## Summary
This PR was done in collaboration with @A-mundanilkunathil and fixes a bug in relation to bound text and it's associated container, where if the text is aligned in any position other than center, rotating the container caused the bound text to rotate around its own central axis instead of rotating around the central axis of the container.

The fix works by:
1. Determining the center points of both the container and the bound text.
2. Applying the container’s rotation angle to the bound text center to obtain a preliminary position.
3. Applying an offset to that preliminary position to determine the correct final placement of the bound text.  

This PR also seems to resolve #5859 where bound text that exceeded the container's maximum height caused the container to extend, but the text itself would render misaligned.


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9b00c6a1-a033-438d-8928-d9331a2d7eaa


### Fix
- `computeBoundTextPosition` in `textElement.ts` has been updated to take into account the position of bound text when it's container is rotated (previously always calculated at angle 0)
- `rotateSingleElement` in `resizeElements.ts` has been updated to use `computeBoundTextPosition` for `x` and `y` placement
- `rotateMultipleElements` in `resizeElements.ts` has been updated similarly to `rotateSingleElement`.
- `updateWysiwygStyle` in `textWysiwyg.tsx` has been updated to correctly set the `x` position
- 'textElement.test.tsx" is updated with tests to account for the changes made to the `computeBoundTextPosition` across all alignments with rotation.

### Note
In `history.test.tsx`, there was a test where bound text and its container were rotated 90° with the text aligned top-left. Previously, this test didn’t account for the positional shift that occurs after rotation. Updates include:
- Adjusting the bound text position to reflect the correct placement at 90°.
- Updating the snapshot to match the corrected behavior.

### Feedback Welcome
We're more than happy to adjust anything as needed—please let us know if there's anything that could be improved or if there's a better way to approach the fix. Appreciate any suggestions or review comments!

cc @Mrazator – would appreciate any feedback you have on this one! Hope all has been going well on your end.


Fixes #8612
Fixes #5859

title: fix: Fix the root cause of `flushSync` flickering
body:
The problem was that browsers have some rounding logic when doing `canvas.style.[width|height] = ...` and `canvas.[width|height] = ...`. Hence, with some setups, conditions like [this](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/d6a934ed196874e50dac498c63e9f2af7c90296d/packages/excalidraw/components/canvases/StaticCanvas.tsx#L54) always returned false. These conditions are there to prevent flickering.

The solution in this PR switches from comparing against the DOM values to comparing against previous values (via `useEffect`).

This PR should fix `flushSync` flickering issues like flickering during alt-duplication and flickering during line editor point addition.

title: fix: aligning and distributing elements and nested groups while editing a group
body:
## Summary
This PR fixes a bug related to the align and distribute actions that prevented their usage while in edit group mode. Previously, when editing the contents of a group, align/distribute actions were disabled for selected elements and/or nested groups.
- both align and distribute actions had a similar structure in organizing selectedElements into groups, although they both seemed to use `getMaximumGroups()` which is also used in `snapping.ts` and used in order to group existing elements in the scene that are not selected.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/57975b69-4849-4a9f-8139-f55102bff86c

### Fix
The logic has been updated to ensure that align and distribute operations work as expected within the context of group editing and can still pass all previously written tests for the align action.
- addition of a new method `getSelectedElementsByGroup` which is actually very similar to `getMaximumGroups` but returns the maximum selected grouping(could be renamed to reflect that).
- this new method is also used in `actionAlign.ts` and `actionDistribute.ts` in their predicates to show only when the tool can be used.

#### Feedback Welcome
I'm happy to adjust anything as needed—please let me know if there's anything that could be improved or if there's a better way to approach the fix. Appreciate any suggestions or review comments!

### Related issue
Fixes #9606 

cc @Mrazator  — appreciate your input on this! Let me know if it resolves what you noticed, and I'm happy to adjust anything if needed.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Improved alignment and distribution actions to better respect element grouping and nested group structures.
  * Enhanced logic for enabling alignment and distribution actions based on grouped selection, providing more intuitive behavior in group edit modes.

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Alignment and distribution now work correctly with elements inside groups and nested groups.

* **Tests**
  * Added comprehensive tests covering alignment behaviors for grouped and nested group scenarios, including group edit mode.

title: feat: capture images after they initialize
body:
Previous synchronous `scheduleCapture` is fine for history (due to applying latest changes on undo / redo), but the captured "added" delta won't contain `fileId` and natural image dimensions, which makes it useless for other use cases (i.e. sync/versioning).

Hence, modifying one more time, so that these asynchronously initialized properties will be on the "added" delta as soon as the image initializes.

Also done:
- further simplifies the image creation in one `createImageElement`
- removes leftovers from image preview and drag-to-create image
- we don't select the placeholder image on image insertion, so the selection is tied to image initialization (and captured together)

title: fix: set cursor to auto when not hovering a point on linear element
body:
Fixes [#9628 ](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/9628)

## The Issue

The issue was occuring in the pointer move event handling logic in Excalidraw. When a linear element (like a line or arrow) is selected, the application prioritizes handling hover events for the selected linear element over detecting hover events for other elements.

**_These are some points where I came to know the problem further_**

1. In the `handleCanvasPointerMove` function, when a linear element is selected, the app was calling `handleHoverSelectedLinearElement` first.

2. The `handleHoverSelectedLinearElement` function was only checking for hover events on the selected linear element and was not allowng hover detection to continue for other elements.

3. Also in the function, there was another check for `this.state.selectedLinearElement` that again was calling `handleHoverSelectedLinearElement` but was not properly continuing with hover detection for other elements.

4. The cursor was only set to `MOVE` if the pointer was over the selected linear element itself, but there was no fallback to check other elements when the pointer was moving away from the selected linear element.


## Explanation of Solution

The issue with hover detection when a linear element is selected has been fixed by modifying the `handleCanvasPointerMove` function in Excalidraw. Here's what the changes do:


1. **Enhanced hover detection for selected linear elements**: In the section where the app handles the case when a linear element is selected, I have added additional logic to:
- First check if we're hovering over the selected linear element (as before)
- If we're not hovering over the selected linear element (hoverPointIndex is -1), check if we're hovering over other elements
- Update the cursor accordingly based on what's under the pointer

2. **Fallback to default cursor**: If the pointer is not over any element, we reset the cursor to the default AUTO type.

## **Demo**

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c1805658-b93d-4406-a448-2b8540715130
author: ashwintemkar

commits.message: fix: set cursor to auto when not hovering a point on linear element #9628

title: feat: various delta improvements
body:
- element delta now contains `version` and `versionNonce`
  - so that we know to which element version the delta relates to
  - so that after applying deltas during time-travel we would end up with exactly the same element versions
  - undo / redo now applies latest changes to both inserted and deleted partials, so that we have the latest `version` and `versionNonce` in both (based on prev and next element snapshot)
  - undo / redo now skips applying `version` and `versionNonce`, since we always need to generate a new one due to collaboration, as it's a new user action
- `points` property for freedraw / linear element is now filtered out from deltas if no points have changed
- updated history snapshots, which now contain `version` (`versionNonce` is stripped)
- added additional delta utils (inner join)
- added runtime validations for version & versionNonce (running in tests & dev)
- exposed `getSceneElementsMapIncludingDeleted` through API

title: feat: line polygons
body: [Link to the icons in Excalidraw](https://excalidraw.com/#json=ibZLCwRLBrCVNEz1qAye7,5-JHv6sl6DC1UzhCsOwW-Q) This PR introduces a new "Loop Lock" feature for line elements. The Loop Lock allows users to lock the start and end points of a line element to create a closed loop. This is particularly useful for creating shapes or paths that need to maintain a continuous loop (e.g. a triangle) The Loop Lock action is visible in the elements panel under the following conditions: 1. Only line elements are selected. 2. All selected line elements have the same loop lock status (either all locked or all unlocked). 3. Each selected line element has at least **4 points**. The Loop Lock action allows users to toggle the loop lock status for all selected line elements simultaneously. When toggled: - If the loop lock is enabled, the start and end points of the line are connected to form a loop. - If the loop lock is disabled, the connection between the start and end points is removed. ## Summary of Changes 1. **Action Definition** (`actionToggleLoopLock`) 2. **Elements Panel** (`Actions.tsx`): The Loop Lock is visible only when all selected elements are line elements with the same loop lock status and at least 4 points. 3. **Type Definitions** (`types.ts`): Added the `toggleLoopLock` action to the list of available actions. 4. **Line Element Editor** (`linearElementEditor.ts`): Updated the logic to handle the loop lock behavior when points are added, moved, or deleted. Loop lock is disabled if the start or end points are deleted as that breaks the loop. 5. **Icons and Labels**: Added icons (`LoopLockedIcon` and `LoopUnlockedIcon`) to represent the locked and unlocked states of the loop lock feature. Updated labels to reflect the toggle action (e.g., "Lock Loop" and "Unlock Loop"). 6. **Restore** will restore loopLock if missing from isLineElement(element) 7. **NewElement**: Added loopLock: false to new Line Element. ## User Experience - **Visibility**: The Loop Lock action is only visible when the conditions mentioned above are met. - **Consistency**: The action ensures that all selected line elements are updated consistently. - **Feedback**: The UI provides clear feedback on the current loop lock status through icons and labels. #### Testing - [x] Verified that the Loop Lock action is only visible when the conditions are met. - [x] Tested toggling the loop lock status for single and multiple line elements. - [x] Ensured that the loop lock behavior is preserved when points are added, moved, or deleted. - [x] Confirmed that the feature does not interfere with other element types or actions. https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/00195f19-9e50-4e67-b118-a89d3890589b

title: feat: Call actionFinalize at the end of arrow creation and drag
body:
Goal of the PR:
- Route through handle pointer up events through actionFinalize for linear elements
- Handle situations where the user by accident creates an invisibly small elbow arrow

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

- **New Features**
  - Improved detection and handling of invisibly small or looped linear and free-draw elements for more accurate editing and restoration.
- **Refactor**
  - Centralized and streamlined finalization and binding logic for linear elements, simplifying user interactions and improving consistency.
- **Bug Fixes**
  - Fixed issues with restoration and selection of imperceptibly small or looped linear elements.
- **Tests**
  - Added and updated test cases to verify correct handling of small and looped elements, and adjusted render expectations accordingly.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
author: mtolmacs

reviews.body:
**Actionable comments posted: 2**

<details>
<summary>🧹 Nitpick comments (4)</summary><blockquote>

<details>
<summary>packages/excalidraw/components/App.tsx (2)</summary><blockquote>

`8996-8998`: **Pass a consistent `source` and verify that the propagated `event` is really the *pointer-up* you want to finalize**

`actionFinalize` now differentiates behaviour based on the `source` (`"ui" | "keyboard" | "api" | …`) and, in the latest refactor, may rely on the optional `event` argument to determine whether the call originates from the final *pointer-up* of a drawing gesture.  
Here you forward `childEvent`, but only when you fall into this particular branch; other call-sites in this component do not (see lines 9122-9123). Please double-check:

1. `childEvent` is guaranteed to be the *last* `pointerup` for the current gesture (not a move/cancel).  
2. The same logical path cannot call `actionFinalize` twice with the same gesture, which would lead to double finalisation.  
3. All other calls to `actionFinalize` in this file follow a consistent convention regarding `source` and `event` to avoid subtle divergence in behaviour.

If any of these are not true, consider normalising the wrapper:

```diff
- this.actionManager.executeAction(actionFinalize, "ui", {
-   event: childEvent,
- });
+ this.actionManager.executeAction(actionFinalize, "ui", { event: childEvent });
```

or introduce a small helper so that every call in `App.tsx` looks identical.

---

`9122-9123`: **Potential missing context: this invocation ships no `event` and no explicit `source`**

Compared with the previous hunk, this call omits both:

```ts
this.actionManager.executeAction(actionFinalize);
```

If `actionFinalize` internally expects at least a `source` (and optionally the pointer event) to decide whether to bind, loop-close, or discard an “invisibly small” element, the absence could lead to divergent behaviour between creation and drag-finalisation paths.

Action items:

1. Confirm `actionFinalize.

title: feat: add `onIncrement` API
body:
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ee3e804a-90ca-48e5-a4f1-533cb8e68450)

- exposed `onIncrement` API with two types of increments emitted
  - `EphemeralIncrement` for all but recorded changes, containing `StoreChange`, which includes **changed elements and changed appState** (emitted everytime there is a change and it's not recorded in history, including remote updates)
  - `DurableIncrement` for recorded changes, containing `StoreChange` as well, plus more granular `StoreDelta` which includes **changed properties on changed elements and appState** 
  
 **Other improvements**
- renamed `change` module to `delta`, since `change` is now used for `StoreChange`
- moved `stored` and `delta` into `@excalidraw/element` to be usable outside editor
- updated snapshot tests to only check for content inside undo / redo stacks
   - not the whole `History` as before, as it now depends on `Store`
- added ability to restore `StoreDelta` (when it comes as DTO from db / network)
- transitioned from committing to store before & after component update -> into committing only after component updates
  - allows to execute in FIFO multiple scheduled actions (`CaptureUpdateAction`) in one component update (i.e. simultaneous local and remote update, or batched remote udpates)

closes #7921, #7923
closes #7925 (partially - `applyIncrement` might be added later, for now there is `applyTo` on `StoreDelta` is sufficient)

docs will be added in #9458, after we stabilize the API


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

- **New Features**
  - Introduced a new state management system for tracking changes to scene elements and app state, supporting granular undo/redo and collaboration workflows.
  - Added support for external consumers to listen for incremental state changes via a new `onIncrement` callback in the API and component props.

- **Improvements**
  - Unified and streamlined update scheduling and history management, improving undo/redo reliability and consistency.
  - Enhanced delta calculation for stable property ordering and more efficient change tracking.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - Ensured correct ordering and emission of ephemeral increments during batched updates.

- **Refactor**
  - Migrated to a delta-based history and store model, simplifying internal logic and public method signatures.

- **Tests**
  - Added and updated tests to verify stable delta outputs, correct history behavior, and event emission.

- **Chores**
  - Updated import paths and re-exports for improved package structure and maintainability.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

title: refactor: remove dependency on the (static) Scene
body:
**What was done**:
- removed static `Scene` methods from everywhere, passing in either `elementsMap` or `scene` (depending on whether we need to inform mutation)
- removed `informMutation` from `mutateElement`, so now it does not inform mutation by default
  - use `scene.mutateElement` or `excalidrawAPI.mutateElement` instead (breaking change)

**Why it was done**:
- so that `@excalidraw/element`, such as `change`, could be used server-side, without providing & updating a global (static) Scene

**Todo**:
- [x] `informMutation: false` is the new default, meaning all mutateElement should be revisited, whether they actually need to trigger mutations or not
- [x] make `ExcalidrawImperativeApi.mutateElement` accessible through the API, if we end up deprecating `mutateElement`
- [x] think one more time about moving the scene inside `@excalidraw/element`, as then the scene could be used independently of the `@excalidraw/excalidraw` package
- [x] fix issues with incorrect labels while rotating (possibly there are more of these)
- [x] fix tests
- [x] merge with latest changes
- [x] double check the mutateElement changes (so that we don't end up in a state where we should inform mutation but don't
- [x] double-check the additional re-renders in snapshots
- [ ] add breaking change to docs next - could be done after we merge this PR
author: Mrazator

title: fix: keep orig elem in place on alt-duplication
body:
fix https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/2109 yay 🎉
fix https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/9402 (regression from https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/9246)

non-goal of this PR: https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/9404

---

This PR fixes the long-standing behavior where if we alt-duplicate an element, instead of swapping the dragged element with the clone, we swapped the original element. This meant that we were effectively modifying the original element (and putting a new one in its position). It also necessitate lots of ad-hoc hacks to account for this.


- added `pointerDownState.drag.origin` which is necessary for the new alt-duplication behavior
- on alt-duplicate we:
  - update `pointerDownState.hit.allHitElements` with the duplicated elements (which are now being dragged in place of the original)
  - update `pointerDownState.drag.origin` which is now used to calculate `dragOffset` for dragging logic. This is necessary because the offset was being calculated from the original elements starting poistion (which we previously kept dragging). Now we drag the duplicated elements, so we recalculate the offset from the cursor position at which we duplicated.
- removed `fixReversedBindings()` and related as they're no longer needed (hopefully)
- removed `duplicateElements.reverseOrder` flag as it's no longer needed
- moved `duplicateElements.overrides` invocation _after_ all duplications and mutations/repairs are done so that the caller has access to the final element values, and can override them as desired
- rest of the changes are just naming changes

title: fix: Add DOCTYPE and XML preamble in exported SVG documents
body:
SVG images exported by Excalidraw contain only the `<svg>` element representing the image, without specifying a DOCTYPE or the XML preamble for older tools to recognize and render images correctly.

This adds that preamble to increase compatibility, following recommendations from SVG 1.1: https://www.w3.org/TR/SVG11/struct.html

I tested this E2E. Exported image:

* Before:

```
<svg version="1.1" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 856.1042326312408 424.0772977941177"  ...
```

* After:
```
<?xml version="1.0" standalone="no"?>
<!DOCTYPE svg PUBLIC "-//W3C//DTD SVG 1.1//EN" "http://www.w3.org/Graphics/SVG/1.1/DTD/svg11.dtd">
<svg version="1.1" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 856.1042326312408 424.0772977941177"  ...
```

Also verified that loading the new SVG document files both in existing Excalidraw version and in the new version works correctly.
author: rubennorte

title: feat: lasso selection
body:
Introducing lasso selection to the editor. This brings users more flexibility and in some cases more accuracy for shape selection.

For the implementation, there's a LassoTrail class that extends the AnimatedTrail. The class is used by App to init, start & update a path, and updates the selection. As a lasso trail is updated, we also have a `lassoSelection` on interactive app state for rendering a bg for lasso path so as to offer some more visual aid.

LassoTrail class relies on the the worker that's located in the same folder so as to offload the intersection and enclosure tests in order not to block the main thread for rendering and interaction.

As a workaround to avoid including any DOM related imports and ops in the worker, we calculate and cache line segments of each visible element and pass to the worker. This ensures that accurate line segments can be computed (the helpers that we need are located in files that also include DOM related things; refactoring to get a clean separation would require too many changes) and used in the worker without said refactors.

close #5263
